signals.headline: ETH ETF Inflows Hit $275M Weekly High (Strongest Since January)** | YES | HIGH
signals.raw:
ETH ETF Inflows Hit $275M Weekly High (Strongest Since January)** | YES | HIGH  
Ethereum ETFs recorded over $275 million in weekly inflows, the highest since January 2026, led by Fidelity’s FETH and BlackRock’s ETHA, coinciding with a 7% ETH price climb to near $2,400 amid rising institutional demand.[2] This marks confirmed institutional return, boosting ETH from ~$2,288 and signaling prediction

signals.headline: Pectra Upgrade Enters Final Testing Phase** | NO | MED
signals.raw:
Pectra Upgrade Enters Final Testing Phase** | NO | MED  
No new confirmation today; Pectra (Prague-Electra) was reported in final testing as of April 17-18, enhancing wallet abstraction and validator efficiency, with ongoing network impacts like gas volatility noted April 16.[3][5] Lacks "right now" resolution to drive immediate markets.[3]

signals.headline: ETH Price Holds ~$2,300 Amid Recovery** | NO | LOW
signals.raw:
ETH Price Holds ~$2,300 Amid Recovery** | NO | LOW  
ETH traded at $2,317.80 on April 20 (down daily but up yearly) and $2,358 on April 17; no fresh surge or drop confirmed today to move predictions.[1][4] ETF inflows provide context but price remains ~52% below ATH.[2]
